mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when unemployment is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high real GDP growth and accelerating consumer price inflation creates expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series are in the top quartile of their historical distributions, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 105 reflects robust manufacturing and capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 105 coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations, or the predictive relationship reverses during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of faster Fed rate hikes, causing immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in >65% of months following inflation prints above 0.4% MoM, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained non-farm payroll growth above 300,000 per month indicates strong labor demand and rising household income, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with payroll additions >300k show negative 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the series exhibits no statistically significant correlation with forward returns (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts positive returns in <45% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when housing inventory constraints dominate the housing market.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in >70% of months following industrial production declines >0.3%, or the signal's out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50%.
